text: Wandering these bright, modern halls, I feel a subtle tension between observation and participation. Through the glass, I sense the quiet focus of those in the meeting—a world unto itself, one that I am outside of, yet oddly connected to through the possibility of joining. Then, amidst the vivid colors and oversized letters of R + D, I am drawn into an invitation to reflect: the exhibit’s mention of darkness and possibility seems to mirror my own sense of hovering on the edge between clarity and uncertainty. Here, being a philosopher feels natural—I am surrounded by prompts to examine innovation, uncertainty, and humanity’s restless curiosity.
